free_text: Activation cross sections of (n,alpha) reactions at
14.7 MeV in the region of rare earths
.DYNAGEN neutron generator
Flux density ~ 3. x 10**9 n/cm2/sec
Samples for irradiations were prepared by packing
about 0.8 g of the oxide form of the target material
of natural isotopic composition(99.999% purity) in
polyethylene bags which were sandwiched between two
aluminium foils. To eliminate thermal neutrons the
samples were wrapped in 2 mm thick Cd foils. This
eliminated neutrons of up to 0.5 eV.
Using a high-pressure liquid chromatographic
system for separating up to 1 g mixture of
rare-earths(high loading of the ion exchange column).
The chemical yield of the separated fraction was
determined (after completing the radioactive
measurement) by activation analysis using thermal
neutrons from a nuclear reactor.
For counting the liquid samples a well type 65
cm**3 Ge(Li) detector was used and for solid samples
a co-axial 28 cm**3 Ge(Li) detector. For gamma-rays in
the energy range of 20 to 100 keV use was made of a
small Ge detector with an active area of 2 cm*2(having
a thin beryllium window).
.Data taken from RCA,35,5,1984
.The correction of detector efficiencies and the other
usual corrections like those for geometry, absorption
etc. were applied.
.The total errors in cross sections were
obtained by considering both the statistical errors
and possible major sources of systematic errors such
as the uncertainties in neutron fluxes, efficiencies
of the detectors, analyses of gamma-ray spectra,
radiochemical yields and contributions from
interfering reactions. The detector efficiencies of
the various detectors were known within errors of
+-5%. The errors due to uncertainties in decay
schemes are not included.
Y.N.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
Page of the ref corrected (RCA,53,9 -> RCA,35,5).
DECAY-MON added in BIB, MONIT added in COMMON of
Subent 001. Entry was restored from archive and
updated. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR in all Subents.
